rulings.Case Title: STOKES, CORY, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS VS. CSE HOLDINGS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Plaintiff'S Application For Order: A) Granting Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement And Provisionally Certifying The Settlement Class; B) Approving Form And Manner Of Notice To Provide To The Proposed Settlement Class And Directing That A Neutral Third Party Give Such Notice To The Proposed Settlement Class; C) Approving Heffler Claims Group, A Neutral Thrid Party, As Claims Administrator; D) Setting A Hearing For Final Approval Of The Proposed Settlement And Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Costs To Class Counsel And Enhancement To Class Representative; E) ...
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, September 23, 2014, Line 5, PLAINTIFF CORY STOKES, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS' Application For Order: A) Granting Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement And Provisionally Certifying The Settlement Class; B) Approving Form And Manner Of Notice To Provide To The Proposed Settlement Class And Directing That A Neutral Third Party Give Such Notice To The Proposed Settlement Class; C) Approving Heffler Claims Group, A Neutral Thrid Party, As Claims Administrator; D) Setting A Hearing For Final Approval Of The Proposed Settlement And Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Costs To Class Counsel And Enhancement To Class Representative. Continued to October 20, 2014. Counsel has not provided data on how many workweeks each of the 57 Account Managers completed, nor has the "distribution formula" been provided to explain the value of each workweek. It is also unclear what will happen in the event that few people sign up and there are funds leftover from the Net Settlement Amount. It appears that the cy pres organization will only receive uncashed checks, not necessarily surplus funds (if any) from the Net Settlement Amount. It is unclear whether the settlement agreement proposes to have the surplus funds be distributed among all class members, be passed to cy pres, or revert to Defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BANCHERO et al VS. AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA AND UTAH INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, September 23, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e) and (f).) As to the first cause of action for declaratory relief, Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to clearly allege what judicial determination is sought. As pled, it is unclear whether an actual controversy exists. As to the second cause of action for breach of contract, Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to clearly identify the specific provisions of the policy that were allegedly breached. As to the third cause of action for bad faith, Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to clearly allege that benefits due under the policy have been withheld and that the reason for withholding the benefits was unreasonable or without proper cause. (Love v. Fire Insurance Exchange (1990) 221 Cal.App.3d 1136, 1151.) The First Amended Complaint does not attach the policy or state the relevant provisions of the policy, and therefore it does not adequately plead that benefits were improperly withheld under the policy. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: S & A CONSTRUCTION, INC. VS. HANYA BARTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Responses To Defendant'S Form And Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 23, 2014, Line 3, Motion To Compel Responses To Defendant's Form And Special Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The unopposed motion is GRANTED. S&A Construction, Inc. shall serve complete, verified responses, without objections, to Barth's Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, and Requests for Production of Documents (Set One) within ten days of this Order. S&A Construction, Inc. shall pay monetary sanctions to Barth in the amount of $1,165.00 within ten days of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EVON CORPORATION VS. JUSTICE INVESTORS,A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration And Staying This Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, September 23, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANTs GEOFFREY PALERMO, JUSTICE INVESTORS, PORTSMOUTH SQUARE, INC.'s Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration And Staying This Action. The motion to compel arbitration and stay is denied. Paragraphs 6.4 and 6.5 (dispute resolution provisions) of the Limited Partnership Interest Redemption Option Agreement ("option agreement") apply to this case. Plaintiff's tort claims implicate "interpretation or enforcement of" the option agreement. The court needs to refer to the option agreement to determine the parties' obligations and those obligations will help determine whether defendants engaged in tortious conduct. The terms of the dispute resolution clauses are broad enough to encompass tort and contract claims. See Buckhorn v. St. Jude Heritage Med. Group (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 1401. In addition, even if defendants had the right to compel arbitration, defendants waived that right. Defendants acted inconsistently with the right to compel arbitration and plaintiff is prejudiced. Plaintiff substantively responded to defendants' discovery and expended resources addressing the parties' discovery disputes. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE CALIFORNIA BY AND THROUGH VS. TAMARA YUROVSKY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motin To Quash Pltff'S Subpoenas Duces Tecum To Airbnb, Inc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 23, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT TAMARA YUROVSKY, LEV YUROVSKY, TATYANA YUROVSKY, LEV AND TATYANA YUROVSKY, AS TRUSTEES OF THE LEV Motion To Quash Plaintiff's Subpoenas Duces Tecum To Airbnb, Inc. Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' motion to quash the subpoenas duces tecum issued to Airbnb, Inc. is DENIED. Airbnb is not a "witness" within the meaning of CCP section 1985.3, thus, Plaintiff was not required to provide a consumer records notice prior to serving the subpoenas. Defendants' other objections based on the right of privacy, the First Amendment to the U.S. Constitution, the attorney work-product doctrine, and the breadth of the subpoenas do not provide sufficient grounds under the facts before the Court to justify quashing the subpoenas. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $6,180.00 because Defendants' positions are not substantially justified. The Judge Pro Tem has limited the sanction to 30 hours of Plaintiff's counsel's time because the Judge Pro Tem is not persuaded by Plaintiffs' assertion that the absence of a separate statement or the case law citations in Defendants' moving papers justify a request for a higher number of hours. Defendants' request for judicial notice of the complaint is GRANTED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM NANCE VS. EVON CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, September 23, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANT EVON CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff alleges standing to maintain this action. Plaintiff alleges that he is a shareholder of defendant at paragraphs 3 and 11-12 of the complaint and defendant failed to transfer the shares. Plaintiff alleges an actual controversy warranting declaratory relief under CCP 1060. Lastly, the court cannot determine whether defendant acted in accordance with or violated the Corporations Code. Defendant argues that defendant was incorporated in 1967 and as a factual matter outside of the pleadings, defendant never filed an amendment of its articles with the Secretary of State electing to be governed by the 1977 amendments. Even if the court grants defendant's request for judicial notice, defendant fails to show that the Corporations Code sections cited in the complaint are inapplicable to this action. Exhibit A of the request for judicial notice refers to an entity named "Evon Garage Corporation." The defendant in this case is named "Evon Corporation." Exhibit B of the request for judicial notice is a print out from the Secretary of State's website. The court can take judicial notice of the website, but cannot accept the contents of the website as true. See Searles Valley Minerals Operations, Inc. v. State Bd. of Equalization (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 514, 519 (affirming denial of request for judicial notice of materials contained on the website pages of the American Coal Foundation and the U.S. Department of Energy); Ragland v. U.S. Bank Nat'l Ass'n (2012) 209 Cal.App.4th 182, 193 (while a court "may take judicial notice of the existence of [a government] report, Websites, and blogs, [it] may not accept their contents as true."). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
